results.prediction: Schedule Policy/Career legal challenge succeeds or fails
results.description: Schedule Policy/Career reclassification of ~50,000 federal positions took effect in March 2026, despite union opposition and public comments, with no reported federal court injunction or ruling on lawsuits as of March 13, 2026.[1][3][5] Agencies are now posting jobs under Schedule Policy (SP) and Schedule Career (SC) designations and conducting reclassification audits, amid criticism from lawmakers and advocates but without judicial intervention mentioned.[1][3] The policy is actively implemented as a 2026 compromise evolving from Schedule F.[1]
